message:
fix(core): harden SQL parse cache against hash collisions; DRY cache helpers

The parse cache previously stored HashMap<u64, Statement> keyed only by
a 64-bit hash of the SQL string. A hash collision could silently return
the wrong cached AST for a different SQL string.

crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs:
- New ParseCacheEntry { sql: String, statement: Statement } stores the
  original SQL text alongside the cached AST for equality verification
- Three new helper methods extracted from duplicated inline logic:
  refresh_parse_cache_if_needed (schema cookie invalidation),
  lookup_parse_cache (hash lookup + sql equality guard),
  insert_parse_cache (bounded insertion, clears at 256 entries)
- cached_parse_single and cached_parse_multi refactored to use helpers,
  removing ~25 lines of duplicated cookie-invalidation logic
- cached_parse_multi gains hot-path fast return for repeated single-
  statement execute() calls (checks cache before parse_statements)
- New test test_parse_cache_execute_uses_single_statement_fast_path:
  injects poisoned cache entry and confirms cached result is used
- New test test_parse_cache_hash_match_requires_sql_equality: verifies
  collision with differing SQL text does not return wrong AST

.beads/issues.jsonl: advance bd-1dp9.6.2 to in_progress

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

This task is not complete until evidence is attached for all three layers:
1. **Unit tests**: deterministic tests proving local semantics/invariants for the changed surface.
2. **E2E scripts**: scenario-level scripts exercising realistic flows and differential behavior where applicable.
3. **Structured logging artifacts**: machine-parseable logs with run_id, seed/config, phase markers, first-divergence indicators (when relevant), and artifact hashes.

Minimum evidence bundle:
- test identifiers + pass/fail summary,
- reproduction command(s),
- output artifact locations/hashes,
- short rationale linking evidence to acceptance criteria.

Gate policy:
- Missing any one of the three layers = bead remains open.
- Evidence must be reproducible on a clean checkout with pinned config/seeds.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.6","title":"Track 6: Extreme Optimization and Performance Parity","description":"## Context
Parity without performance is unacceptable; optimization without proof is dangerous.

## Goal
Achieve performance parity targets using strict profile-first, isomorphism-preserving optimization loops.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Baselines + flamegraphs + opportunity matrix published.
- Only score>=2.0 optimizations implemented.
- Every optimization has isomorphism proof + golden checksum verification.
- Final perf parity report includes p50/p95/p99 and throughput deltas.

## Success Criteria
Epic completion requires all child beads to satisfy their structured acceptance criteria, including explicit unit/property coverage, deterministic e2e evidence, and structured logging/metrics validation.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every child bead in this epic defines concrete unit/property test scope with deterministic pass/fail thresholds and invariant IDs. 2. Critical-path end-to-end scenarios are implemented as reproducible e2e scripts (fixed seeds, replay commands, artifact bundles) across happy-path and adversarial-path behavior. 3. Structured logging and metrics contracts are mandatory for all child outputs (trace_id, run_id, scenario_id, seed, timing, outcome, error context) and validated by CI conformance checks. 4. Epic completion requires a machine-readable evidence manifest linking child unit/e2e/log artifacts to acceptance outcomes, plus an explicit fallback/rollback plan that preserves concurrent-writer defaults.","notes":"2026-02-23 WhiteKite follow-up: Updated acceptance contracts for bd-1dp9.6.2, bd-1dp9.6.4, bd-1dp9.9.2, bd-1dp9.9.3, bd-1dp9.9.4, bd-1dp9.9.5 plus remaining baseline regressions (bd-2qr3a, bd-2qr3a.5, bd-2y306.2, bd-2y306.5, bd-3bw.5, bd-3plop.5) so quality-gate parser requirements are explicit: unit/property tests, deterministic e2e fixed-seed replay, and structured logging/metrics with trace_id/run_id/scenario_id. Verification: target/debug/backlog_quality_gate_runner ... => overall_pass=true, total_failures=0, regression_failures=0; artifacts/backlog-quality-gate-e2e/repo/report-latest.{json,md}.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:02:23.366156385Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-02T04:59:49.902520723Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated","isomorphism","optimization","parity100","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:57:54Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:57:54Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:57:54Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:57:54Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:57:54Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":196,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Track 6 operating notes:
- Apply extreme optimization discipline without semantic drift.
- Optimization work is gated by profile evidence and behavior-preservation proof.

Design choices:
- Mandatory loop: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix -> single-lever change -> golden/isomorphism proof -> reprofile.
- Only execute opportunities with Impact*Confidence/Effort >= threshold defined in T6.1 policy.
- Keep SQL-path and storage/MVCC-path optimization streams separated for attribution clarity.

Test/logging requirements:
- Benchmark artifacts include p50/p95/p99, throughput, and memory deltas.
- Golden output checksum validation before/after each optimization set.
- Isomorphism proof notes attached per optimization change set.

Definition of done:
- Performance parity target met with explicit non-regression evidence and CI guardrails.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:11:25Z"}]}

","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:31Z"},{"id":203,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress slice: captured baseline for SSI edge-discovery hotspots and evaluated one optimization lever, then reverted due measured regression (proof-preserving discipline). Baseline (before lever): incoming active_readers/4 1.56-1.60us, /8 3.44-3.47us, /16 6.67-6.74us; outgoing committed_writers/4 1.48-1.49us, /8 3.17-3.25us, /16 6.11-6.18us. Lever tested: replace per-call page index structure in discover_incoming_edges/discover_outgoing_edges from BTreeMap to HashMap with preallocation. Result: statistically significant regression across all sizes (roughly +21% to +52% latency). Change was fully reverted; codebase left with no performance-regressing behavior changes. Validation after revert: cargo fmt --check -p fsqlite-mvcc; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-quietwillow-mvcc cargo clippy -p fsqlite-mvcc --all-targets -- -D warnings; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-quietwillow-mvcc cargo test -p fsqlite-mvcc ssi_validation::tests::test_ -- --nocapture; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-quietwillow-mvcc cargo test -p fsqlite-mvcc begin_concurrent::tests::test_fcw_deterministic_tiebreak_lower_txn_id_wins -- --nocapture.","created_at":"2026-02-13T20:52:41Z"},{"id":204,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress slice (TurquoiseMarsh, 2026-02-20): implemented a single-lever SSI edge-discovery hot-path optimization in crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/ssi_validation.rs by deleting interval_end/intervals_overlap helper dispatch and inlining overlap checks with precomputed committing interval bounds (u64) in discover_incoming_edges/discover_outgoing_edges.\n\nValidation (rch):\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-mvcc ssi_validation::tests::test_ -- --nocapture  => PASS (39/39)\n- rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fsqlite-mvcc --all-targets -- -D warnings => PASS\n- rch exec -- cargo bench -p fsqlite-mvcc --bench mvcc_bench -- ssi_edge_discovery\n\nBenchmark comparison (criterion):\n- incoming/active_readers/8 improved from ~[4.569..4.765]us to [4.311..4.513]us (p<0.05, improvement detected)\n- incoming/4, incoming/16, outgoing/{4,8,16}: no statistically significant regression (all no-change)\n\nWorkspace status snapshot:\n- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets => PASS\n- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings => FAIL due unrelated pre-existing cast lint in crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/codegen.rs:2914 (usize->i32), outside this bead scope\n- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check => FAIL due unrelated formatting deltas in crates/fsqlite/src/lib.rs (and import order expectations before local rustfmt pass)\n\nEvidence contract note: this slice covers unit + perf evidence for one optimization lever; deterministic e2e script + structured artifact manifest for bead closure still pending.","created_at":"2026-02-20T08:40:39Z"},{"id":205,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Evidence addendum (TurquoiseMarsh, 2026-02-20): added deterministic E2E + structured logging artifacts for storage/MVCC workload validation.\n\nE2E command (offloaded):\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-e2e --test mvcc_concurrent_writers -- --nocapture\n\nResults:\n- PASS: 15/15 tests in mvcc_concurrent_writers\n- Hot-page contention scaling report and disjoint-partition scaling report emitted in test output\n\nStructured artifacts:\n- Run log JSONL: artifacts/perf/bd-1dp9.6.3/e2e_mvcc_concurrent_writers_log.jsonl\n  - run_id: bd-1dp9.6.3-20260220T085926Z-977866\n  - scenario_id: mvcc_concurrent_writers\n  - seed: 42\n  - status: 0\n- Raw E2E output: artifacts/perf/bd-1dp9.6.3/e2e_mvcc_concurrent_writers_output.txt (19832 bytes)\n\nNote: attempted realdb-e2e workload run first, but remote rch environment lacked untracked golden fixture DB files from sample_sqlite_db_files;  {"id":"bd-1dp9.6.5","title":"T6.5 Isomorphism-proof harness and golden checksum automation for optimization changes","description":"Operationalize optimization safety: every performance change must carry proof that behavior is preserved. ","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:32Z"},{"id":208,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## bd-1dp9.6.5 Closure Evidence: Isomorphism-proof harness and golden checksum automation

### Summary
Full parity across all 10 proof invariant classes, including all 4 mandatory invariants (golden_checksum, error_codes, null_propagation, aggregate_semantics). SHA-256 golden checksum automation with CI gate enforcement.

### Implementation
- crates/fsqlite-harness/src/isomorphism_proof.rs (orchestrator module)
  - ProofInvariantClass enum (10 classes: row_ordering, tie_break, floating_point_precision, rng_determinism, golden_checksum, type_affinity, null_propagation, error_codes, aggregate_semantics, window_function_semantics)
  - IsomorphismVerdict (Parity, Partial, Drift)
  - IsomorphismProofRecord for machine-readable proof records
  - 20 individual checks across 10 invariant classes
  - assess_isomorphism_proof() orchestrator + file I/O + triage_line
  - 17 unit tests

### Evidence
1) Harness unit (17p/0f): cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --lib -- isomorphism_proof
2) Harness integration (11p/0f): cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --test bd_1dp9_6_5_isomorphism_proof
3) Toolchain determinism (58p/0f): cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --lib -- toolchain_determinism
4) Ratchet policy (25p/0f): cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --lib -- ratchet_policy
5) E2E script: PASS (111 total, 0 failed). Artifact: test-results/bd-1dp9.6.5-isomorphism-proof-verify.json
","created_at":"2026-02-13T20:34:59Z"}]}
